reason: The text explicitly describes an upcoming famine of seven years that will affect the entire land of Egypt and surrounding lands. Joseph's interpretation attributes this future calamity to God's revelation and plan: 'שֶׁ֤בַע שָׁנִים֙ הֵ֔נָּה יִהְי֕וּ שֶׁ֖בַע שְׁנֵ֥י רָעָֽב' (seven years of famine). The text emphasizes this is not an individual affliction but a widespread disaster: 'וְנִשְׁכַּ֥ח כָּל־ הַשָּׂבָ֖ע בְּאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרָ֑יִם וְכִלָּ֥ה הָרָעָ֖ב אֶת־ הָאָֽרֶץ' (all the plenty shall be forgotten in the land of Egypt, and the famine shall consume the land). The passage then narrates the beginning of this public catastrophe: 'וַתִּרְעַב֙ כָּל־ אֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֔יִם' (all the land of Egypt famished). This is clearly a supernatural disaster revealed by God that will devastate the general population.
